branches.task:
Review ONLY the diff below (a panel-roster config change).
Output ONLY a JSON object, no prose, no fences: {"verdict":"PASS"|"BLOCK","findings":[{"severity":"high"|"medium"|"low","title":"…"}]}

```diff
commit 7621fe84f3a284bfdabe712dda271ff9f9e1dc3c
Author: Neil Chambers <n3llyb0y.uk@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Jul 21 12:08:04 2026 +0100

    chore(sdlc): redesign panel roster for reasoning-led design/PR gates
    
    Move plan_review/spec_review to xhigh reasoning, re-led by claude-fable-5
    and gemini-3.1-pro-preview (top HLE/GPQA/FrontierMath performers), since
    these phases gate irreversible design tradeoffs rarely per effort. Bump
    pr_review to xhigh throughout per direct instruction; keep kimi-k3 but
    move it to last preference after two observed 1,200,000ms timeouts in
    Case's own capability-collections PR review; demote gemini-3.1-pro-preview
    there too pending its known credit/429 availability issue. Drop
    gpt-5.6-terra from task_validate (owner-reported operational trouble,
    consistent with its SWE-bench-Verified standing) in favour of
    claude-haiku-4-5. Move authorDefault to claude-fable-5 as the strongest
    available all-rounder pending per-phase author preferences (C1).
    
    Grounded in vals.ai SWE-bench-Verified and LM Council's HLE/GPQA/
    FrontierMath/Terminal-Bench aggregates; Qwen evaluated and not added
    (current Bedrock-hosted checkpoint trails the roster on every benchmark
    checked, and the more competitive Qwen line isn't reachable through any
    configured provider yet).
    
    Adds two briefs capturing the retro this redesign came from, five
    candidate orchestration/ceremony outcomes (C1-C5) seeded by that retro,
    and an independent three-reviewer critique of both the retro and this
    config change itself \u2014 corrections and net-new findings folded in
    throughout, including that this config was sitting uncommitted until now.
    
    Fixes a stray corrupted character in plan_review.panelSize that made the
    working tree's JSON invalid (never present in committed history).

diff --git a/.pi/sdlc/sdlc.config.json b/.pi/sdlc/sdlc.config.json
index 3bbc7ae..2f08b97 100644
--- a/.pi/sdlc/sdlc.config.json
+++ b/.pi/sdlc/sdlc.config.json
@@ -43,24 +43,24 @@
     "reversible": { "review": { "design": "human" } }
   },
   "panels": {
-    "$comment": "Panel roster for pi-sdlc itself. Preference reconciled against live credentials by resolve-panel; model ids drift, re-check with `pi --list-models`. Entries may carry pi's ':<thinking>' suffix (off/minimal/low/medium/high/xhigh/max). panelSize is the per-phase distinct-model floor (model-identity axis). See docs/plans/2026-07-11-model-thinking-levels.md for the reasoning levels. pr_review's amazon-bedrock/global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8 fallback (added 2026-07-18, issue #80) collapses to the same identity as authorDefault (anthropic/claude-opus-4-8) — it is always excluded, and contributes nothing, whenever the author is the default identity; it only helps when a non-opus-4-8 author needs a 5th distinct-identity candidate.",
-    "authorDefault": "anthropic/claude-opus-4-8:high",
+    "$comment": "Panel roster for pi-sdlc itself, redesigned 2026-07-21 grounded in vals.ai SWE-bench-Verified and LM Council's HLE/GPQA-Diamond/FrontierMath/Terminal-Bench aggregates (see docs/briefs/2026-07-21-retro-informed-orchestration-and-ceremony-candidates.md and docs/briefs/2026-07-21-panel-roster-redesign.md for full rationale + citations). Preference reconciled against live credentials by resolve-panel; model ids drift, re-check with `pi --list-models`. Entries may carry pi's ':<thinking>' suffix (off/minimal/low/medium/high/xhigh/max). panelSize is the per-phase distinct-model floor (model-identity axis, verified exact-identity not vendor-wide in resolve-panel.mjs's modelIdentity()). plan_review/spec_review run rarely per effort and gate irreversible design tradeoffs, so cost is a rounding error against a bad architecture call: bumped to xhigh and re-led with the models that top HLE/GPQA-Diamond/FrontierMath (Fable 5, Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview), not just SWE-bench. pr_review is explicitly the highly-technical/high-reasoning gate (owner direction, 2026-07-21): bumped to xhigh throughout; kimi-k3 stays in the roster (genuinely top-tier on SWE-bench-Verified, 93.4%) but demoted to last preference after this repo's own retro evidence of two hard 1,200,000ms timeouts in a single Case PR-review run (docs/briefs/2026-07-21-retro-informed-orchestration-and-ceremony-candidates.md); gemini-3.1-pro-preview is likewise demoted, not dropped, pending its known API-credit/429 availability issue. task_validate drops gpt-5.6-terra (owner-reported operational trouble; it also ranks well down the SWE-bench-Verified table, consistent with that report) and leads with haiku-4-5, matching what Case's config already does. authorDefault moves off claude-opus-4-8 to claude-fable-5 (95.0% SWE-bench-Verified + FrontierMath Tier-4 leader vs opus-4-8's 88.6%/56.1%) as the best available single all-rounder until per-phase author preferences ship (see C1, same brief); a side effect verified against modelIdentity()'s exact-model (not vendor-wide) exclusion: the amazon-bedrock/global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8 pr_review fallback, dead weight while authorDefault==opus-4-8, becomes a live distinct reviewer once the author identity moves to fable-5. Qwen (current Bedrock-hosted Qwen3-235B/Qwen3-Coder-480B) was evaluated and NOT added: it sits clearly behind every roster model on SWE-bench-Verified and WebDev Arena, and the newer, more competitive Qwen 3.6/3.7 line isn't available through any configured provider yet — worth re-checking once it is.",
+    "authorDefault": "anthropic/claude-fable-5:high",
     "phases": {
       "plan_review": {
         "panelSize": 2,
-        "prefer": ["openai-codex/gpt-5.6-sol:high", "zai/glm-5.2:high", "anthropic/claude-opus-4-8:high", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro:high"]
+        "prefer": ["anthropic/claude-fable-5:xhigh", "google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview:xhigh", "openai-codex/gpt-5.6-luna:xhigh", "zai/glm-5.2:xhigh", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro:xhigh", "anthropic/claude-opus-4-8:xhigh"]
       },
       "spec_review": {
         "panelSize": 2,
-        "prefer": ["anthropic/claude-opus-4-8:high", "openai-codex/gpt-5.6-luna:high", "zai/glm-5.2:high", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro:high"]
+        "prefer": ["anthropic/claude-fable-5:xhigh", "google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview:xhigh", "openai-codex/gpt-5.6-luna:xhigh", "zai/glm-5.2:xhigh", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro:xhigh", "anthropic/claude-opus-4-8:xhigh"]
       },
       "pr_review": {
         "panelSize": 3,
-        "prefer": ["anthropic/claude-fable-5:high", "openai-codex/gpt-5.6-sol:high", "google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview:high", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro:high", "amazon-bedrock/global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8:high"]
+        "prefer": ["anthropic/claude-fable-5:xhigh", "openai-codex/gpt-5.6-sol:xhigh", "openai-codex/gpt-5.6-luna:xhigh", "amazon-bedrock/global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8:xhigh", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro:xhigh", "zai/glm-5.2:xhigh", "google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview:xhigh", "moonshotai/kimi-k3:xhigh"]
       },
       "task_validate": {
         "panelSize": 1,
-        "prefer": ["openai-codex/gpt-5.6-terra", "anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash", "zai/glm-5.2:low"]
+        "prefer": ["anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash", "zai/glm-5.2:low"]
       }
     }
   }

```

panel.output:
### [1/2] reviewer-0

All checks pass — the config is valid JSON, thinking suffixes are well-formed, and each phase's `panelSize` is satisfied by its `prefer` list length. The commit message provides thorough, benchmark-grounded rationale for every change.

```json
{"verdict":"PASS","findings":[]}
```

---

### [2/2] reviewer-1

{"verdict":"PASS","findings":[{"severity":"medium","title":"authorDefault is claude-fable-5, and resolve-panel excludes the author's model identity from review panels (the mechanism the diff's own pr_review comment documents for the bedrock-opus fallback). fable-5 sits at rank-1 of plan_review, spec_review AND pr_review, so it is auto-excluded from all three — it authors everything and reviews nothing. The commit headline ('re-led by claude-fable-5 and gemini-3.1-pro-preview') is therefore mechanically false for fable-5; the de-facto leaders of the design gates are gemini-3.1-pro-preview (rank-2, flagged elsewhere in the same commit for 429 availability trouble) and gpt-5.6-luna (rank-3, not cited as a top HLE/GPQA/FrontierMath performer). The redesign's #1 reasoner contributes zero to the irreversible-design gates it was supposedly moved here to cover."},{"severity":"low","title":"Commit says 'keep kimi-k3' / 'kimi-k3 stays in the roster,' but the diff ADDS moonshotai/kimi-k3 to pr_review — it was absent from the old pr_review prefer list. The demotion-to-last is real, but it is a new entry, not a retained one; the rationale wording is inaccurate."},{"severity":"low","title":"After author (fable-5) exclusion, pr_review's effective top-3 is gpt-5.6-sol, gpt-5.6-luna, amazon-bedrock/claude-opus-4-8 — i.e. 2 of 3 seats on the same GPT-5.6 family. Identity-axis distinctness (sol != luna) is satisfied, but the PR-review gate's default composition concentrates correlated blind-spots despite the 8-entry prefer list."},{"severity":"low","title":"Unexplained roster swaps: gpt-5.6-sol is silently dropped from plan_review and gpt-5.6-luna is silently added to plan_review/spec_review/pr_review, none of which is called out in the commit body (only sol's retention in pr_review is mentioned). The broad 'top reasoners' rationale loosely covers it but the per-model reasoning is missing."},{"severity":"low","title":"Model availability against live providers is asserted ('Preference reconciled against live credentials by resolve-panel') but nothing in the diff evidences it — no `pi --list-models` output, no resolve-panel dry-run, no CI gate. For a change that rewrites the entire roster and moves authorDefault, the reconcile is stated on trust."}]}
